summary: Fair soundboard with uneven volume captures a fiery ’69 performance highlighted by an inspired Dark Star.
review: The recording is a sound‑board capture that suffers from inconsistent levels – parts are almost inaudible while others are blaring – and includes oddly isolated short drum‑solo tracks. Musically the show shines, especially the open, jazzy Dark Star and a spirited rendition of The Other One, with the band clearly on fire in classic ’69 fashion.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (4‑5 stars) praising Dark Star and overall energy, but a few note serious volume issues, pulling the average down to about four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Volume swings from inaudible to blaring; occasional flat‑soundboard feel. A 12–17 second drum solo is split into its own track, which some listeners find unnecessary.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic ’69 set with a particularly open and jazzy Dark Star; overall flow feels inspired despite the uneven recording.

band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer humorously calls Phil’s bass moment a “fart” but treats it as a notable musical interlude.
band_member_comments.Drums: A very short drum solo (12‑17 seconds) was isolated into its own track, which some listeners consider unnecessary.
